reviews.review_text:
finally finished KH1! remember getting stuck in the jungle when I was kid. The story, the gameplay, the memories... it's all still the same. Thanks, Square Enix, for this game. No matter the price, it’s worth every penny to buy back those memories.

and when kh4 -_-
Pretty decent compilation but unfortunately they didn't include Kingdom Hearts: Sora's Big Maths Adventure from the Casio FX-82ES Plus Scientific Calculator, so I have to give it 4 out of 5 stars.

TL;DR - first game bad. I finished it so I can see the main story resolve and have a fundamental understanding of the lore before moving on to the better games by popular vote. 

I started this game because I've heard nothing but praise for the series throughout my whole life. I've never played it nor understood much of the lore, so I thought this collection would be a good way for me to experience it. 

I wouldn't recommend the first game, because it's clunky, the combat is repetitive and meh, the story elements with the Disney characters feels a bit cringe. I don't like how the puzzles are hard in a stupid way and not in a clever way. The game feels like a first attempt at a new type of game in the early 2000's, which I suppose that's what it is. I can see how nostalgia will smooth over the rough patches of this game, but I don't have that, so it's just a mid game at best.

That being said, I'm still gonna finish the first game because I did get curious about the plot and want to see it through, and my distaste for the overall gameplay doesn't overpower that need to see the story through. PLUS, I heard from many sources that the series really picks up starting at the second entry, so I can't pass the first game just to skip to the sequel. Besides, it's only a 30 hour game, not as bad as other JRPGs.

I typically do not write out long reviews for games, but this game holds such a special place in my heart ( pun intended) that I must write more than just "game good". This and every game in the series has to be one of the most well thought out combat and story ever created on God's green earth. I kid you not that it is an emotional rollercoaster with such high highs and very sad lows. If you ever get the chance to play this game I believe that you will have the time of your life.
The childhood nostalgia that I feel when opening this game just hits me right in the depression!

nothing like the nostalgic experience of forcing yourself through the tarzan and alice in wonderland worlds, finally getting back to traverse town, having your game crash, realizing you hadn't saved in eight hours, and rage quitting the game again
Playing this game with my cousin is one of my earliest childhood memories I have. Playing it now brings back some very heart warming memories

Oh boy-
This is a defining purchase.
This is a life-molding purchase.
This is the greatest purchase you will ever make.

Kingdom Hearts is my favorite video game series for a reason. This has a collection of some of the best games of the entire series (NOT LOOKING AT YOU RE:COM), You get KH1 which still holds up to this day being charming, mysterious, and somehow tearjerking while you are staring at donalds goofy ass face.

KHII is the best game ever made, I need not to elaborate. If you are having any doubts get to the midgame with the battle of.... you know... and then come tell me it wasnt the best game you ever played.

358/2 is an amazing game for real, but these are just cutscenes. BUT BE SURE TO WATCH THEM BECAUSE THE UNLOCK ♥♥♥♥ IN RE:COM FOR SOME REASON???

Re:Coded? I dont know that one. I think when i see those letters in that order I kinda go temporarily blind, burned that one from my memory.

In all honesty, i probably have over 1000 hours in the KH series between all the way back with the PS2 to today. Do yourself a favor, do your KIDS a favor, and show everyone this game that will take the time to listen. A core tenant for my humanity, I LOVE KINGDOM HEARTS<3

Steam Deck compatible in every way. Yet to run into any issues at all. This has always held such a close spot to my heart and honestly I have nothing bad to say about this game. Love it on the Steam Deck it truly brings back solid memories from my teen years.
best ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game ever i would let anyone who plays this game take advantage of me.

This game is GREAT for getting kids started in playing combat type games. I have played this series for a very long time and it's just as good now as it was then. Its a very great for exploring and casual play as well for a progression based game as you can level grind to get over the level requirements to get easy boss fights if you don't like a challenge you just have to grind quite a bit to do it.  It doesn't have as many bugs as I thought but so far I'm playing KH1 and this is really hitting my nostalgia buttons like Spyro did. It has about the same level of gameplay if you've played it. (Just a personal opinion though, use a controller it's easier. the camera with the mouse is kinda eh.)

Kingdom Hearts 1.5 + 2.5 ReMIX is a beautiful collection of 6 titles. There's 4 full games and a collection of cutscenes that retell the game events from the games 358 days and Re: Coded.

10/10
it fun and and it challenge's you and really good replay ability also has some awesome characters

I will review the games in order of how much I like them (top is the best) and before you go ballistic, there will be some honest criticism 

Kingdom Hearts 2: I always judge sequels based on how much they improve. KH2 improves through 1 a lot. An 8.8/10

Birth by Sleep: The tale of the whiny teen, the idiot and the girl is plenty of fun. The system it uses allows for dozens of playstyles, even though the fun way to play is to spam Meteor with Terra. Its major drawback  is that you need to go through the same worlds 3 times. I'd prefer a way to quickly switch characters. I give it a 8/10

Kingdom Hearts: I still like the game, but revisiting it after a dozen years seems very...weird. Very blocky. It wins mostly through worlds than through gameplay. Also, it may be the hardest in the list. I give it a 7.6/10.

Chains of memories: Interesting gimmick, but waaay to grindy. Also it's completely inconsequential story-wise, because Sora doesn't even remember the game's events. I give it a 6/10

This game has been apart of my life since it first came out in 2002. Honestly one of the best game franchises I have ever played and I cannot recommend this series enough. Kingdom Hearts 2 will always hold a special place in my heart specifically. Give it a chance, it might change your life too 😌

You get two PS2 classics, a controversial remake of a GBA game, and a PSP standout in this collection plus HD cutscenes of two DS games (can be easily emulated if you fancy trying them)

Even though some aspects of these games can be janky or absurd, they can also be some of the most fun, challenging, and rewarding games ever released, especially 1 and 2 on higher difficulties (although beware if you have anger issues or a low tolerance to BS difficulty spikes)

If your at all curious about Kingdom Hearts this is the perfect purchase, It comes with 4 entire games (two full length RPGs, one smaller side game, and also BBS) and two "movies" of the cutscenes from the Nintendo DS games. 

Kingdom Hearts 1 & 2 are generally considered the peak of the franchise, (gameplay wise) and although 1 does show it's age in a few places its still a fantastic game once you get a handle on the command system.

Then there's Re:Chain of Memories, which has a unique (and highly controversial) card based combat system that massively changes the standard gameplay formula. Newcomers to the series often end up stuck on this game since the game doesn't do the best job at really showing how the cards and deckbuilding work. While I really recommend pushing through if you really don't like it you should at least watch a cutscene compilation online before going continuing. 

Despite what the menu order shows the next game is Kingdom Hearts 2, playing 358/2 Days first actively ruins parts of the story of 2 (and KH2 was released first anyway). To put it shortly KH2 is the best game in the series easily, while there are some minor things I wish were different it's the best overall package of a game. The story starts off confusing as if you missed something in between games but that's the intended experience and things get explained overtime.

Then the 358/2 Days movie, it mostly exists to explain some of the story that went unexplained in 2. I do recommend playing the actual DS game (and this goes for the other movie too) since there are a couple smaller things that get skipped in the movie.

There's also the Re:coded movie, although this is the one game/movie that you can actually mostly skip if you wanted. Its the second game that mostly retells KH1's plot and only really has like one part at the very end that relates to later games. (though I highly recommend playing the ds game here since the combat is 2nd best in the series)

Last there's Kingdom Hearts Birth By Sleep, and it's just kinda ok. Story is pretty good but the combat is sloggy and just often annoying. The game is separated into 3 playthroughs, each with their own playable character and story. While a interesting idea in practice you end up doing the same thing with all 3 stories fairly often so it just drags on. 

Genuinely might be the best value you can get for this price on steam, and it regularly goes on sale. I mean, I have nearly 200 hours at the time of writing and while I have beaten all the games I've only actually 100%ed 1 & 2 (but am close on Re:CoM). 

Also, after your first playthrough of each game you should try them on the hardest difficulty, it goes from mostly a button masher at times to (for most games) a intricate combat system with all the tools you get over the playthrough. Along with that the superbosses are great if your looking for a real challenge to test your game knowledge on (just not in BBS, those bosses are actually horrible).

This is the best package of Kingdom Hearts games. To begin with, you get the much more grounded and simplistic story of 1, with ok gameplay and the ability to use the analog stick to look around instead of the bumpers like in the original game. Then you get 2 with an interesting story and solid gameplay. Then you cap it off with a middle of the road Birth by Sleep.
The music is top tier.  This game is just childhood wrapped in a blanket.  Also Disney and Final Fantasy
It's a great way to get into the KH series, but replay the cutscenes after you beat each game cuz you'll see nice callbacks and stuff!

Kingdom Hearts 2 Final Mix is one of those games I can come back to anytime and still have an amazing time. The combat feels insanely smooth, the music is unforgettable, and some moments genuinely hit me emotionally even years later. Beating the optional bosses felt so rewarding too. This game is pure comfort and nostalgia for me.

These games were my entire childhood. I fell in love with it since I was a kid to the point that when i grow up i thought it was some fever dream I had. Being able to re play these beautiful games after so long bring me pure happiness and nostalgia.

You know honestly I'm pretty pissed I have bought each of these games probably at least 3 times each, always at full price of course. But damnit, it's just too convenient, especially with a steam deck.

I've laughed, cried, smiled, got angry, this franchise has brought out almost every emotion I could describe. Though only TWO of the "games" are basically 2-3 hours movies it was still fun to watch and feeling the same emotions if I were to be playing.

I will admit some of it still has it flaws like the game feeling a bit too overwhelming and annoying specifically in KH1 but that's honestly just  a skill issue on my part, but regardless EVERYONE has struggled their first time in Atlantica.

The game play difference in the games are a big jarring at first but its still fun to learn different combat styles (Even COM) even if I don't like it, since it has still convinced me enough to play through it or want to buy the original!

The story is amazing, perfect even. It gets really congested later on in the series but in the beginning it is the perfect balance between sad and happy. The overall meaning of the story is also up to interpretation as well.

No game is perfect and this one isn't either but if you were to ever start your first JRPG series this would be the one!
good game, good music, good story, its like fanfiction at times, try it out!!!!!

This is the absolute best way for anyone looking to get into the series/revisit it. This collection has 4 games (6 if you use Melon Mix) and 2 movies of DS titles including KH1, KH Re:Chain of Memories, KH2 and KH BBS all remastered and in one collection. 

Kingdom Hearts at it's core is a game about friendship and what strong willpower and belief in oneself can push somebody to accomplish. You primarily play as Sora, a boy from the Destiny Islands looking to save/fight beside his friends in the battle between light and darkness. The story is confusing and somewhat convoluted until it isn't. Once you've finished each game, everything that was confusing beforehand suddenly makes sense. It's what makes a series replay so genuinely satisfying, everything is given to you upfront and it's only later that you understand the context and what objectively happened.

I had played through the first 5 games in the series before taking a break and this collection was the perfect way for me to come back to it. I can't recommend these games enough to anyone, I'm not a big Final Fantasy or Disney fan, however the story that's crafted in these games ensures you don't need to be in order to genuinely engross yourself and enjoy the story. I'm finally finishing my playthrough of the series now and I'm excited to revisit this collection a few years down the line to replay it.
I've been playing this game for over 20 years! It never fails to lift my spirits to the extent that I've bought the entire collection at least 3 times!

I've liked this game ever since I beat it on PS2 back in the day. Was happy to catch the bundle on sale since I only played the first one all the way through. The second one , I didn't get much time to play when it first came out. Looking forward to playing through the whole collection.

KH is a classic. The first game is mechanically the worst game. Save points, rough controls, and a couple of crashes mid dungeon will easily deter 2026 players from completing the beloved classic. Expect boss spamming ultra powerful moves while Sora and crew run endlessly in circles - both due to a lack of direction and in-combat damage mitigation.
Played Kingdom Hearts BEfoRe.. Fun Game!! I defently  recommend this ... Playing this again anticipated for Kingdom Hearts 4 ..
The first collection of Kingdom Hearts games. Just as good as they've been the past 20 years.
im mad that i lost 1 hours of gameplay inside the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ stomach and the game crashed as soon as i was gonna save other than that i like the game

As cheesey as this storyline is, it's still a solid action JRPG and runs super well on PC. Brings me back to my childhood. Chain of memories is still one I tend to skip seeing I am not much into card collecting, but the other games are still great!

It's one of the most interesting franchises I've got myself into, It's a game that you have to like from the get go and have to take seriously, if not...

You. Will. Not. Get. It. 

I think. anyways it's got it's good and bad, first the good, then the bad

The good
One of the best stories in videogames i've seen in my life, it's interesting, fricking confusing goddayum, and an interesting combat system between the six games (4 actually) and a good cast of characters and villains!!! I love it

The BAD
THE CAMERA OH GOD THE CAMERA, how is it that as a kind of hack and slash game you give it the worst camera imaginable!!!!! I don't remember any other bad experiences, besides VANITAS LINGERING SPIRIT DAYUUMN MAN THAT BOSS WAS JUST HORRIBLE, AND YOU TOO MYSTERIOUS FIGURE IM SEEING YOU

I cheated at least 30 of the 197 achievements, I will not waste like 40 hours completing EVERY REPORT OF EVERY SINGLE CHARACTER FROM BIRTH BY SLEEP AND LEVELING THE ARENA TO LEVEL 30 GO SUCK IT

My ranking:

1st place we have Kingdom Hearts 2
2nd place we got Kingdom Hearts Birth by Sleep (It would actually be in first place if it werent for the horrible camera and other things)
3rd place we got Kingdom Hearts 1
4th place we got Chains of Memories because the gameplay is AWFUL DOG WATER, the story is goated tho but i hated the gameplay, moreso Riku's part, because spamming sleighs with sora was fun.

368/2 day is goated and good
and Re: Coded is the most horrible thing i watch in my life, the last 30 minutes are the best part of it, the rest is just derivative slop

Doesnt matter how long it goes, KH1 and KH2 will always be amazing.

Specially KH2, there's still no game that surpases this one in the entire saga.
This game is such a piece of art. This is genuinely THE best game of all time (I have a few hundred hours on this game on playstation and grew up with it).

The parts included are fun, and good enough to justify buying the game, but honestly this is one of the laziest ports I've seen in a long time. It's not unusual for a Japanese game to be poorly optimized for mouse and keyboard, but in this particular case it's like they didn't even test it before release and it amazingly is still awful. You will not be using a mouse in the lvl 3 gummi missions because the slightest mouse twitch rotates the camera. You CAN use a mouse when doing the puzzles or in the gummi editor, but it's incredibly sensitive to any mouse movement and can be exceedingly difficult. The most frustrating part of this is that that there was no actual reason they should be as terrible as they are. It's not an instance of the controls being too complex or specialized to map to keyboard and mouse, the developers just didn't put in any effort to modify the controls for users using a mouse, I'm assuming they just mapped joystick movement to mouse movements and called it a day, when even amateurs know that this always works poorly without adjustment. Lack of effort also shows in how the games  all share a single key configuration. It's a much smaller problem than the mouse issues, but it just further shows that no effort went into adapting the port for PC when any effort at all would have produced a better result.  I'm left with only two possible conclusions, given that this is a major studio production with no shortage of skilled staff, either the devs were too lazy to adjust it or (more likely) management didn't want them investing too much time in the port. Either way, the lack of effort really shows. It looks even worse when you consider that two of the games they didn't even port beyond cutscene compilation, though I'm a little more sympathetic since porting the games would have required adaptation from a dual screen console. It is worth nothing again that this is baked by major studios that would have had enough resources to do so, though, It does at least bit having to look up a synopsis online and there's no guarantee I'd have actually enjoyed the 3DS gameplay anyways. Again, I'd still recommend it for kh 1 and 2 alone,  but I just get the heavy impression that someone put a cap on development time before any actual adaptation for the PC could happen, save for only the most basic changes to make (most) of the game playable on keyboard and mouse.  Overall, the games are enjoyable and a little AHK wizardry cna alleviate some of the issues, I'd wait for it to go on sale before purchasing. Partially to save yourself some change for a title Disney and Square Enix know they can milk for full price just a little longer than other games, but mostly just to spite them for not putting in the effort for a game that would be ridiculously easy to justify doing so.
I really don't need to explain why this game series is worth your time and money. Everything I can say had already been said.

As a beginner that has been recommended this game by veteran players, it's pretty fun. The game doesn't walk you through the game step-by-step, which is in my opinion fun. Because you get to explore at your own pace, figure out mechanics and figure out the storyline as you progress. Which is really interesting, I've always liked old games for this reason. The difficulty is just right and enough to truly test your skills.
It's Kingdom Hearts but now I can mod it with very little issue! Too bad they wont give Days or Coded a remaster but whatever I GUESS.

I dont even know how to properly explain what Kingdom Hearts means to me without sounding insane.
This series has genuinely been a part of my life for as long as I can remember. I have tattoos for it. I own multiple physical copies of the games for different systems because for some reason every time Square rereleases them I go “yeah alright one more time.” I have literally bought consoles just to play Kingdom Hearts before. Thats not even an exaggeration
Kingdom Hearts 1.5 + 2.5 Remix honestly feels less like a game collection and more like somebody handing me a box full of my childhood memories.
And yeah, I know people online love making jokes about Kingdom Hearts. The confusing story. The belts and zippers. The giant shoes. The fact that Donald Duck is standing next to Sephiroth while Goofy is talking about the darkness. I get it. But I also think people forget just how special these games actually are
Especially Kingdom Hearts 1
There is just something about the first game that no other entry in the series fully captured again. Not that the later games are bad because honestly I love almost every single one of them in some way. KH2 is probably the best gameplay wise. Birth by Sleep ruined my mental health in te best way possible. 358/2 Days still emotionally beats me to death every time I think about it. KH3 made me cry as a grown man and I dont care who knows that
But KH1 is different
KH1 feels weirdly lonely sometimes. In a good way
Traverse Town feels like a place you saw in a dream once. Destiny Islands somehow makes me nostalgic for being a kid even when I was literally a kid playing it. Hollow Bastion still has one of the coolest atmospheres in any game I’ve ever played. The whole game has this quiet mysterious feeling to it where half the time you dont fully understand whats happening but emotionally ou completely understand it anyway
And the soundtrack man. Good lord
Yoko Shimomura genuinely made one of the greatest game soundtracks ever. Dearly Beloved alone could probably make me emotional instantly if you played it near me unexpectedly. Simple and Clean is one of those songs where the second it starts playing my brain immediately gets launched back like 20 years. Hearing it after finishing KH1 still hits exactly the same
Also I dont care what anyone says, KH1 combat still rules
Yeah its clunkier than K2. Yeah the platforming can be kinda jank sometimes. But thats part of why I love it honestly. The combat has weight to it. Magic actually feels insanely useful. Exploration mattered. Finding secrets mattered. Finding all the dalmatians felt more imporant to me as a child than some modern games entire stories
And the worlds actually felt dangerous
Wonderland confused the hell out of me as a kid. Deep Jungle feels like a fever dream now. Monstro legitimatelystressed me out every single time I got there. I miss when the Disney worlds felt mysterious and disconnected from reality instead of just replaying the movie plot scene for scene
And Hollow Bastion??
Still one of the greatest sections in gaming history to me
The Riku fight. Losing the Keyblade. Beast joining your party. The music. The atmosphere. Fighting your way back up after basically losing everything. That entire part of the game is unbelievable.
Also this series somehow gets away with dialogue that should absolutely sound stupid but instead goes insanely hard.
“You’re stupid.
Still iconic
And honestly the ending of KH1 destroyed me as a kid. Like actually emotionally devastated me. The music kicking in while Sora and Kairi are reaching for each other as the door closes is permanently burned into my brain forever.
“I’ll come back to you, I promise."
Are you kidding me dude
People say Kindom Hearts is confusing and honestly yeah at this point it absolutely is. There are like seventeen versions of Xehanort running around and every charcter has a nobody, somebody, heartless, dream version, sleep version, data version, and probably another version hidden inside a different version.
And somehow I still eat it up every single time
Because underneath all the insanity, Kingdom Hearts is weirdly sincere. Thats the thing I think I love most about it. The games are cheesy sometimes but theyre never cynical. They fully believe in friendship and hope and staying connected to the people you care about even when everything is falling apart around you
That stuff hit hard when I was younger
Hits even harder now honestly
And this collection specifically is just absurd. KH1 Final Mix. KH2 Final Mix. Birth by Sleep. Re of Memories. The Days cutscenes. Re. Just an absolutely stupid amount of content
I honestly think Kingdom Hearts shaped my taste in games permanently. Probably my taste in music too. Maybe even storytelling in general. No other game series feels quite like this one does
Its messy. Its emotional. Its awkward sometimes. Its confusing. Its sincere to the point of almost feeling embarrassing sometimes
I love it so much
Kingdom Hearts isnt just my favorite game series ever. At this point it kinda feels like a piece of who I am.
10/10
Donald still refuses to heal me.
loved these games since i was a little kid, great for nostalgia and still hold up as good games. recommend.

have physical copies of KH1 and KH2 for the PS2, so much nostalgia and content for an early 2000s rpg, if you enjoy DMC or FF etc any type of hack an slash/rpg of the sort, this series has it all, even a card combat system if you enjoy that more.... also the music is peak

The game is unplayable. I tried playing Birth by Sleep and it keeps crashing. I scoured the net for any solution and tried them all and it still locks up in loading or crashes during cutscenes. As it is DO NOT BUY THIS GAME

while I've had no issue with some of the games in this bundle my one biggest issue is with Kingdom Hearts 2.  Besides the graphical bug in the menu that happens if you use a controller, which is an easy fix if you just use the mouse and keyboard. No my issue is right after a boss fight if you don't skip the cutscenes you will run into a huge chance of the game crashing and wasting painful time having to refight a boss.

Got through all of Kingdom Hearts 1 with little issue (game is [i]fine[/i] but not great and has aged kind of poorly if you don't have nostalgia, if you're wondering.) but starting Chain of Memories has every cutscene and very often the gameplay turn everything black. Support requests are unhelpful. Unplayable garbage port, do not purchase as it is now. If you do, go ahead and check that the games function before the refund window ends. 

Will try to see if I can get something to work, but other companies have functional video games that I can play instead.
This game is worth it for the ports of 1 and 2 alone. Chain of Memories is okay and at the time of review I have not even touched Birth by Sleep yet.
Best franchise I've ever played to this day, these games are just so special for me and i'll never be bored of playing them.

will freeze pc trying to exit game, and will also always mis ID monitor resolution and will force itself out of fullscreen into a tiny window mode at its own convenience or if something else happens on your system. Better to rip online or even better yet play the originals on their own hardware or emulated over this.
Kh1 will crash every 30 min. Kh2 has a menu bug that makes it nearly impossible to play. This is the worst port I've ever seen.

Cousin originally introduced me into kingdom hearts by buying me it for my christmas present when i was in 5th grade and I have loved it ever since. The games have helped me through a lot especially my depression and has provided a ton of comfort to me especially Sora he has been a big comfort character.

SOOO much better than the switch version holy christ. 
Having the option to choose the controller display is lovely & whilst the combat is the most fluid ive ever seen it never feels overcomplicated. power scaling in kh1&2 is soo amazing the keyblade ALWAYS feels cool but NEVER overpowered. its just suuuch an amazing balance. i will always prefer the staggered MP system in KH1 to the timer in KH2 though. also first time players should know that 358/2 Days and Re:Coded do not have playable remakes and are just compilations of cutscenes. still, i think 2 numbered titles, 2 sizable sidegames with multiple campaigns, and 2 cutscene compilations is well worth the price when the games are this good. I love kingdom hearts, SOO glad we got a steam port with achievements though i wish the achievements were per game idk how theyd do that. Square Enix give us kingdom hearts 4 im begging
Prolly one of the best bang for your buck deals on steam, Seven campaigns and two movies all included.

It's exactly like you probably remember. They updated some of the stuff, but it's still like you remember. You should get a controller for some of the games. You could use a keyboard and mouse but it's not ideal.
I still don't understand the story but it brought me back to my fetushood

its a yes and a no, i am in hollow bastion KH1 and the game keeps crashing while going through doors and after i have done something to progress like the library, emblem, gravity chests, moving blocks its annoying but i have to save any time i make progress which means backtracking and hoping that it doesn't crash on the way to save.
absolute classic, plays really smoothly on pc, controller highly reccomended

So let's be clear: you're here for Kingdom Hearts 1 and 2. Chain of Memories is a 30 hour game with 3 hours of (admittedly decent) story, Birth By Sleep is... definitely mediocre compared to the two main-series entries, and 358/2 and Re:Coded are movies.

The ports of 1 and 2 are good, with two main problems, neither of which ruins them. First, quite a few FMVs were never remastered from the original Playstation 2 graphics and so are extremely low-rez and look like garbage on toast. Secondly, the sound mixing works in such a way that whenever several sounds overlap so they should be quite loud, the system automatically attenuates them to near-silence, which is very strange. This can be mitigated somewhat by setting your master volume to 1 and just turning up your headphones, but that you have to do that is kind of obnoxious.

Overall, for games old enough to seriously consider marriage and children, Kingdom Hearts 1 and 2 hold up pretty well. The zones are tiny by modern standards and the voice acting is a little stilted, but they're still excellent games.

was on speical and grabbed all of them . best choice in a long time . Loved KH1 and KH2 100%ed both working on BBs and CoM feels tedious but enjoyable as a long time Hardcore Achievement/Mount Hunter in Both FFXIV online and WoW Retail . This feels like a breeze ! PLEASE LIKE OR REWARD <3 <3
Only play with mods for QoL - Devs didnt care about actually making the changes everyone would hope for.
good value for money, and KH1 - COM - KH2 is a great trilogy of games, all you need from this series

KINGDOM HEARTS FINAL MIX:
Lore & music are masterpiece.
Movement & combat system sometimes clunky.

KINGDOM HEARTS Re:Chain of Memories:
I find the card combat system are unique & fun but require a bit grinding.
Lorewise are okay.

KINGDOM HEARTS 358/2 Days:
Basically a movie, can finish in 2 hours.
Great storytelling.

KINGDOM HEARTS II FINAL MIX:
Combat are upgraded from Kingdom Heart 1 but sometimes annoying enemies especially bosses, but drive form are fun to use.
The best world design are on Timeless River episode.

KINGDOM HEARTS Birth by Sleep FINAL MIX
Finished storyline on Terra, end of Ventus and not started on Aqua.
First of all, new combat system are okay but the camera and aim control are inconsistent, it tested my patience instead of skills.
Enemies are annoying plus with that camera control, I having second thought if I want to finish this game.

Will update once finish other series.
Best way to play it, once I got the mods set up and running, I'll never go back.
it is a great game, really takes you on the full experience
PEAK GAME AND PEAK FRANCHISE AND PEAK STORY AND PEAK CHARACTERS AND PEAK EVERYTHING.
Delightful game if you are into pain and suffering
It's 4 amazing JRPG's and 2 movies. Really nothing to complain about.
Incredibly buggy and crashy port,
too much money, the gummi ship segments are AWFUL and it crashes sometimes for me :(
I love Kingdom Hearts, and now that it is on steam and not  ̶E̶p̶i̶c̶ ̶G̶a̶m̶e̶s̶ ̶L̶a̶u̶n̶c̶h̶e̶r̶ I can have so much more fun, and being on PC, Mods Mods Mods
maybe not everyones fave but its still a fantastic series

These games were my childhood. As sad as it is, I used to save up my pocket money and birthday money so I could get any of the consoles that had new KH games as soon as possible after they released. I must've played through KH1 dozens of times, and KH2 a fair few times as well (though not quite as many). When the collections came to PS3, I played them again, and on PS4. And now I've played them again on Steam. So, clearly, I have a problem.

[h2]Kingdom Hearts 1.5[/h2]
[b]Time to Complete[/b] - 42.4h
I did two playthroughs for this one - one on beginner for unchanging armour, which wasn't a speedrun as such, but because I knew all the room layouts and where to go next from all the times I played through it as a kid, I ended up unintentionally getting the speedrun achievement as well despite watching all the cutscenes as well. I ended up doing this as a 100% playthrough since I'd got to the end anyway.

And then a proper speedrun on Proud just for the difficulty achievements. With the rest of the games I stuck with the higher difficulty for everything, but Proud + Unchanging Armour just seemed painful, so I noped out of that.

I do love this game, but the more you play through it, the more you realise some of the design choices are quite obtuse, and it's not always clear where you should be going if you haven't already done it before. Especially in Deep Jungle I think.


[h2]Re;Chain of Memories[/h2]
[b]Time to Complete[/b] - 46.1h
Done completely on Proud for both characters. This is most peoples' least favourite in this series, as it departs from the usual combat in favour of a weird card based action hybrid thing. I mean, it's [i]fine[/i] but it's also my least favourite and one I'd skip playing again if it wasn't for the full collection being in one achievement list on Steam.

Combat starts of annoying when your cards are limited, but once you unlock enough and can just mash Y to create and use lots of Sleights the combat then becomes fairly trivial. Riku's combat is more fun as it's properly balanced, but grinding him to level 99 is mind-numbing.


[h2]Kingdom Hearts 2.5[/h2]
[b]Time to Complete[/b] - 49.3h
Played through entirely on Critical. This one is probably the best in the collection, combat is fun and dynamic, and reaction commands made most fights fairly easy if you pay enough attention. Some of the Data Organization fights are a bit painful on Critical, and if you haven't played the games much before it might be better to to a quick speedrun on Beginner before tackling them.


[h2]Birth By Sleep[/h2]
[b]Time to Complete[/b] - 65.8h
Fully completed all character on Critical. 
I really enjoy Birth by Sleep, personally. Melding commands to make your deck of moves is pretty fun. With Aqua and Ven, the combat is pretty easy as their dodge commands are really good so you can just dodge around until you get a good opening to attack, so honestly Critical with them was fine, even the secret bosses. I don't think a higher difficulty really matters for those two, especially if you opt for surge cheesing.

Terra on the other hand, is rather frustrating. People talk about the annoyance of Mysterious Figure, but honestly he's not hugely different on higher difficulties. I actually struggled more with the two arena bosses as they both had moves which would lock you enough to stop you using cure, but would hit you after your protection from Once More/Second Chance ran out. I'd never tried them on Critical with Terra before (on PS4 I just went Beginner for the Reports), but they were my stopping point far more than Mysterious Figure.

Overall though, I do enjoy completing all the reports with each character. None of them take too long and if you enjoy minigames (which I do) then it's fun doing them all three times each. Some people find that element frustrating, but I think it's fun.


[h2]Overall Thoughts[/h2]
Yeah, I just love these games. I'm not sure how much of it is nostalgia influencing my thoughts, but I would happily play through them again on another platform to get all the achievements as I love hunting them down.

The full collection also played great right out the box on Steam Deck. My Steam Deck is completely vanilla (don't even have Decky) and I never encountered a single issue with any game in the collection on default settings for both the Deck and the game.
